steps.title: Speed, Velocity and Acceleration
steps.content:
**Speed** is how fast an object moves — it has no direction. **Velocity** is speed in a given direction (a vector). **Acceleration** is the rate of change of velocity.

Average speed: v = d/t

Acceleration: a = Δv/t

steps.title: Speed vs Velocity
steps.question: A car travels 60 km north in 1 hour. Which statement correctly describes its motion?
options: Its speed is 60 km/h and its velocity is 60 km/h north
Its speed and velocity are both 60 km/h — direction doesn't matter
Speed and velocity are the same thing
Its velocity is 60 km/h but its speed cannot be determined
steps.correctFeedback: Correct! Speed is the magnitude (60 km/h) and velocity adds the direction (60 km/h north). They have the same numerical value here but velocity specifies direction.
steps.misconceptionFeedback: Speed is a scalar (magnitude only). Velocity is a vector — it includes direction. A car going 60 km/h north has speed = 60 km/h and velocity = 60 km/h north.
steps.bestSource: AQA GCSE Physics — Forces and Motion

steps.title: Explore Motion
steps.instruction: Adjust the speed and observe how the object moves. Notice how changing direction changes velocity but not speed.
